subtitle: The workflow shift in AI-native development.
cover_note: Every two years the place where your effort actually matters climbs one step higher.
cta_headline: Which step are you standing on?
cta_body: Word, context, rig, or loop? In 2026 the unit you own is the loop. Don't just read it -- close one this week.

slides.caption: You're not getting better at prompting. The skill didn't improve -- it moved up the stack.
Prompt -> context -> harness -> loop. The unit of work you own rises one step every two years.
Rewording hits diminishing returns, a curated context window still can't act, and a 100-line rig has no cycle to run in.
The loop: plan -> act -> observe -> verify -> correct -- with a verification gate and a stop condition.
The harness is the nouns (the rig + its sensors). The loop is the verbs (the act -> observe -> verify -> retry cycle).
Outside, in, on the loop, and the flywheel. 'In the loop' is the trap -- you become the bottleneck.
Generation throughput rises while validation stays flat. The fix: pull verification into the inner loop.
Stripe: ~1,000 -> 1,300+ PRs/week with zero human-written code. SWE-bench Verified: 12.47% -> 76.8% on a fixed harness.
A clear goal, the tools to iterate, one machine-checkable feedback signal, and a stop condition.
Run a verify->correct loop, build it on a managed runtime, then platform-engineer it with gates.
